step_by_step_thinking:
Resilience refers to the property of returning to its original shape after being bent or stretched. When discussing orthodontic wire, resilience describes how easily the wire returns to its initial form post deformation. Energy storage capacity relates closely to the wire’s ability to deform elastically and return to its starting configuration upon removal of external stress—essentially storing strain energy within itself before releasing it immediately afterward. Hence, the correct option describing the characteristic related to energy storage capacity among those listed would be B: Resilience.
\boxed{B} Based on the properties discussed regarding orthodontic wires needing to efficiently return to their initial shapes post-deformation to effectively apply and release stored therapeutic stresses, resilience stands out as crucial for energy storage capability.
